content: We can frame Self-Refine as a single-perspective foundational iteration framework, and integrate multiple-perspective inconsistency sampling from LLM self-reflection mechanisms to build a hierarchical self-refinement system: first generate multiple inconsistent candidate outputs from different solving perspectives, then let Self-Refine iteratively refine each candidate and cross-evaluate across perspectives to reach a better final output, without requiring any additional training, supervision or external test cases. This hybrid system solves the key limitation of vanilla Self-Refine of getting stuck in local optima from its single self-feedback perspective.
